reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between position, velocity, and time. To confirm this, I will increase the time and observe the change in position.
Increasing time did not change the position, suggesting the initial position might be affecting the result. I will now increase the initial_position to see if it changes the position.

The position increased by 10 meters when the initial_position was increased by 10 meters, while velocity and time remained constant. This supports the hypothesis that position is determined by the sum of initial_position and the product of velocity and time.
